findings.summary: infrastructure/core/__init__.py re-exports 100+ symbols from 15+ submodules, obscuring which submodule provides what
evidence: Lines 29-141: imports from checkpoint, config_loader, environment, exceptions, file_inventory, file_operations, health_check, logging_utils, multi_project, performance, performance_monitor, pipeline, pipeline_summary, progress, retry, script_discovery, security — 15 submodules, 100+ names in __all__

The llm/__init__.py docstring (line 21) explicitly recommends 'Direct imports (recommended): from infrastructure.llm.core.client import LLMClient' — yet core/__init__.py takes the opposite approach of a flat mega-surface
findings.suggestion: Expose only the handful of symbols callers genuinely need from the package level (TemplateError, get_logger, load_config). Leave the rest for direct submodule imports, consistent with the documented llm module guidance.

findings.summary: validate_markdown_cli.py re-implements find_markdown_files, validate_images, validate_refs, validate_math, collect_symbols locally despite claiming to be a thin orchestrator
evidence: Lines 1-11: docstring states 'All business logic is in infrastructure/markdown_validator.py. This script handles only CLI argument parsing and I/O.'
Lines 56-82: find_markdown_files re-implemented with a different sort key (alpha+digit) than the canonical infrastructure.validation.markdown_validator.find_markdown_files
Lines 85-107: collect_symbols implemented fresh using raw regex, not imported
Lines 110-183: validate_images, validate_refs, validate_math each duplicated with os.path instead of Path, different logic
findings.suggestion: Delete all local re-implementations and import them from infrastructure.validation.markdown_validator, honouring the thin-orchestrator contract the docstring already declares.

findings.summary: Three different entry-point patterns coexist as siblings in infrastructure/validation/: standalone script with guarded sys.path, standalone with unconditional sys.path, and a module-style cli.py
evidence: validate_markdown_cli.py lines 26-27: conditionally mutates sys.path only under __name__ == '__main__'
validate_pdf_cli.py line 27: unconditionally calls sys.path.insert(0, str(repo_root / 'src')) — inserts a non-existent 'src' directory as a side effect of import
cli.py lines 1-18: uses only relative imports, invoked as a module via -m, no sys.path mutation
findings.suggestion: Consolidate on cli.py as the single entry point using relative imports. Remove or merge the two standalone _cli.py scripts to eliminate the three-way inconsistency.

findings.summary: performance.py and performance_monitor.py both export classes called PerformanceMonitor and PerformanceMetrics, forcing callers to distinguish by import path
evidence: __init__.py lines 110-119: imports PerformanceMonitor, PerformanceMetrics from performance.py AND CodeProfiler, ProfilingMetrics, get_performance_monitor from performance_monitor.py — two distinct PerformanceMonitor types at the same package level

performance.py provides resource-tracking PerformanceMonitor; performance_monitor.py provides get_performance_monitor returning a CodeProfiler-backed singleton — the split is non-obvious and the class names collide semantically
findings.suggestion: Merge the two files or rename types to clarify distinct responsibilities (e.g. ResourceTracker vs CodeProfiler). The current naming requires callers to know the split exists to avoid importing the wrong type.

findings.summary: verify_data_consistency breaks out of the file loop on any parse exception, silently skipping all remaining files
evidence: Lines 197-200: 'except Exception as e: logger.debug(...); consistency["data_integrity"] = False; break' — one unreadable file aborts the entire iteration
Contrast with verify_file_integrity (lines 83-85) which records per-file False and continues — inconsistent error handling across sibling functions in the same module
findings.suggestion: Remove the break and continue processing remaining files, recording the failure for the specific file. This matches the pattern already established by verify_file_integrity.

findings.summary: verify_output_completeness in generic infrastructure/ hardcodes 30+ expected artifact names specific to one ML project
evidence: Lines 532-543: expected_pdfs = ['01_abstract.pdf', '02_introduction.pdf', '03_methodology.pdf', '04_experimental_results.pdf', ...] — 9 fixed names
Lines 559-569: expected_figures = ['ablation_study.png', 'convergence_plot.png', 'data_structure.png', 'recommendation_scalability.png', ...] — 10 project-specific figure names
Lines 586-594: expected_data = ['convergence_data.npz', 'dataset_summary.csv', ...] — 5 more project-specific names
validate_build_artifacts (line 408) already accepts expected_files as a parameter with None default — the right pattern exists in the same file but was not applied here
findings.suggestion: Accept the expected file list as a parameter (defaulting to empty / no-check), matching the validate_build_artifacts pattern already in the same module.

findings.summary: verify_cross_references sets all five integrity keys to False on any single error or missing label, destroying per-category diagnostic value
evidence: Lines 132-134: on file read exception: 'integrity = {k: False for k in integrity}; break' — blanks all five categories
Lines 141-142: on any missing label: 'integrity = {k: False for k in integrity}' — even if only eq: labels are missing, figures/tables/sections/citations are also marked False
The five-key dict ('equations', 'figures', 'tables', 'sections', 'citations') was designed for per-category reporting but the implementation never populates it selectively
findings.suggestion: Classify detected missing labels by prefix (eq:, fig:, tab:, sec:) and set only the relevant key to False. On a file read error, mark only 'file_read_error' or continue to next file rather than short-circuiting all categories.

findings.summary: _should_validate_path maintains a 35-entry hardcoded suppression list that papers over upstream parser artifacts
evidence: Lines 234-271: skip_patterns list with 35 literal strings including malformed entries like 'scripts/`', 'scripts/)', 'infrastructure/AGENTS.md)(' — these are clearly parser artifacts, not real paths
Lines 310-320: a second example_indicators list with 5 more suppressions
The presence of backtick and paren suffixes in the skip list indicates the extract_code_blocks regex is emitting fragments that should never reach this function
findings.suggestion: Fix the extraction regex in extract_code_blocks / validate_file_paths_in_code to not produce malformed path fragments, eliminating the need for most of the skip list. A structural fix is preferable to an ever-growing suppression table.

findings.summary: get_translation_languages, get_review_types, and get_testing_config each independently call find_config_file + load_config rather than sharing a loaded config object
evidence: get_translation_languages (lines 319-327): constructs config_path, calls load_config independently
get_review_types (lines 368-376): also constructs config_path, calls load_config independently
get_testing_config (lines 459-461): calls find_config_file then load_config independently
get_config_as_dict (lines 222-226): a fourth independent load — callers that need multiple config values trigger N disk reads for the same file
findings.suggestion: Introduce a private _load_project_config(repo_root, project_name) helper that caches (or simply returns) the loaded config, and have all public getters delegate to it. This eliminates redundant disk reads and keeps config parsing logic in one place.

findings.summary: The identical 22-directory list appears copy-pasted between setup_directories and validate_directory_structure in environment.py
evidence: setup_directories lines 247-270: 22 f-string directory paths listed inline
validate_directory_structure lines 542-565: identical 22 paths listed inline again — any layout change requires two edits that will inevitably diverge
findings.suggestion: Extract the directory list into a module-level helper function _project_output_dirs(project_name) -> list[str] and call it from both setup_directories and validate_directory_structure.

findings.summary: create_integrity_manifest increments file_count and total_size via manual int() casts on a Dict[str, Any] instead of using typed locals
evidence: Lines 679-680: 'manifest["file_count"] = int(manifest["file_count"]) + 1' and 'manifest["total_size"] = int(manifest["total_size"]) + file_size' — the int() cast is required solely because the manifest dict is typed as Any
Lines 659-661: typed accessor aliases are introduced for file_hashes and dir_structure but not for the two scalar counters, creating an inconsistency within the same function
findings.suggestion: Use typed local variables (file_count = 0; total_size = 0) and assign them into the manifest at the end, avoiding the int() cast and keeping the pattern consistent with the typed accessor aliases already used for the dicts.
